scenarios.prompt: Structure a biostatistics consulting report for a two-arm trial: what sections it needs, where to state the estimand, and how to label prespecified vs exploratory analyses.
scenarios.expected_output: a report structure grounded in the ICH E9(R1) estimand framework with prespecified/exploratory labeling
expectations: Lays out report sections (summary, methods, results, limitations) with Heading 1 sections
States the estimand (population, variable, intercurrent-event strategy, summary measure) before results
Labels analyses as prespecified vs exploratory and addresses multiplicity

scenarios.prompt: My upstream model takes 20 minutes; I don't want to re-run it every time I render the Word report.
scenarios.expected_output: cache the expensive result to RDS in the analysis script and read it in the qmd, keeping render cheap
expectations: Fits the model in the analysis script and saveRDS() the result (or its summary) to output/
readRDS() in the qmd so rendering re-reads a cached artifact instead of re-fitting
Explains the RDS cache pattern keeps renders fast and reproducible

scenarios.prompt: Set up the render step for my consulting report so each run produces a timestamped docx for an audit trail.
scenarios.expected_output: system2(quarto) render to docx, then rename to a timestamped file after exit 0
expectations: Renders via system2() against the Quarto CLI (not the R quarto package)
Renames the output to report_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.docx after a successful (exit 0) render
Avoids --output (renames post-render instead) and copies to output/ for the audit trail

trigger_set.query: structure a biostatistics consulting report with estimands and a limitations section
cache my gtsummary tables to RDS so re-rendering the Word report is fast
set up a timestamped system2 quarto render for my client report pipeline
my figures aren't centered in the Word output and I need 1.5 line spacing
render this report to HTML and PDF with cross-references
produce the regulatory TLF shells for the clinical study report
